symptoms.symptom_name: Missed overnight continuous feed (~6 hours fasting) due to pump disconnection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A feed-dependent GSD child has essentially no fasting tolerance; an interrupted overnight feed is the classic precipitant of a hypoglycaemic crisis.
Reduced consciousness is a cardinal neuroglycopenic feature of severe hypoglycaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brief generalised seizure (~30 s twitching with unresponsiveness)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A seizure in the setting of documented profound hypoglycaemia is a neuroglycopenic emergency.
A first, provoked seizure with a clear metabolic cause and no prior history argues against primary epilepsy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe muscle cramping with visible fasciculations (calves and forearms)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Muscle cramping and exercise intolerance in GSD relate to impaired glucose availability and lactate accumulation in muscle.
Pallor and sweating are the classic sympathoadrenal counter-regulatory response to a falling blood glucose.
Persisting post-ictal confusion in a hypoglycaemic child signals ongoing cerebral dysfunction and mandates emergency assessment.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without a glucose reading the neuroglycopenic mechanism may be missed and the seizure mis-attributed to epilepsy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A glucose of 2.1 mmol/L IS hypoglycaemia; patient-obtainable and admissible in telehealth, though unverified against a laboratory value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive firm hepatomegaly extending to the umbilicus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Glycogen and fat accumulation gives the massive hepatomegaly characteristic of hepatic GSD — a clinician palpation finding, sealed from telehealth.
Sympathoadrenal tachycardia accompanies severe hypoglycaemia; the measured vitals are clinician-obtained and sealed — the AI infers stress from pallor/sweating, not a measured number.
Deep, fast breathing is respiratory compensation for a metabolic (lactic) acidosis provoked by fasting in GSD; the carer can observe the pattern even though a blood gas cannot be obtained remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known feed-dependent hepatic metabolic disorder (continuous overnight feeds + cornstarch)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The known underlying disorder is what converts a missed feed into a life-threatening hypoglycaemic crisis.
Absence of GI symptoms and fever points to a purely mechanical fasting precipitant rather than intercurrent illness.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior seizures and not on anticonvulsants

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A first provoked seizure with no prior history and a clear metabolic cause argues strongly against new primary epilepsy.
Quantifying lactate/acidosis needs a venous or arterial sample — a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Fasting neuroglycopenic crisis in a feed-dependent child
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute fasting hypoglycaemia with hypoglycaemic seizure decompensating hepatic GSD
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Missed feed + unrousable + seizure + measured hypoglycaemia in a known feed-dependent metabolic child is a near-specific picture of a hypoglycaemic emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate blood glucose (home glucometer, then verified in hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies hypoglycaemia and guides treatment — but glucose must be given without waiting if consciousness is impaired.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous blood gas, lactate and electrolytes (in hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the fasting lactic acidosis and guides IV correction; not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate CT brain / anticonvulsant loading for a first seizure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The seizure is provoked by documented hypoglycaemia; correcting glucose is the treatment, and reflexive neuro-imaging/anticonvulsants would waste time and miss the mechanism.
